guidance: New discovery sweeps should PRIORITIZE architecture families first published AFTER last_exhaustive_crawl (focus on big-conference / journal / arXiv output since that date) while remaining free to surface anything missed in ANY earlier era, language, or field. Update this file (add a crawls[] entry and bump last_exhaustive_crawl) at the end of every exhaustive sweep. See ../DISCOVER_MODELS.md for the sweep prompt and ../HARVEST_SOURCES.md for the running list of hubs/zoos/prior-art datasets to draw on (we aim to be a superset of their union). DEDUP via TorchLens graph_shape_hash (architecture fingerprint; see ../HARVEST_SOURCES.md + dedup_report.py). PRIOR-ART ALREADY HARVESTED (2026-06-18): Younger (2406.15132) and Neural Architecture Retrieval (2307.07919) were diffed and added ZERO new named families (their space is already subsumed) -> do NOT re-harvest them expecting gains; ONNX Model Zoo + GitGraph (TF1-era) + PapersWithCode added ~104 (mostly the pre-2014 / ONNX-only / bespoke-repo long tail that library-only sourcing misses). LESSON: pair library enumeration with hub + prior-art + non-library-repo harvesting; library-only sourcing is the #1 blind spot.

axes: domain (vision, NLP, speech, RL/control, graph, time-series, recommender, scientific ML, ...)
paradigm (logic/threshold, PDP/connectionist, neuro-symbolic, vector-symbolic, fuzzy, spiking, evolutionary, ...)
applied-modern (recommender/CTR/tabular, neural operators, implicit-neural-reps, neural processes)
adversarial missed-family hunt (cross-lab hostile auditors, non-English literature explicitly included)
crawls.method: Cross-lab (Claude Opus + OpenAI Codex) sweeps driven to a family-level clean-zero, then a hostile adversarial red-team that still surfaced ~150 more genuine families (folded in: 103 catalog-adds + 58 hand-built classics).
crawls.notes: Most comprehensive sweep to date. NEXT sweep should focus on architectures published after 2026-06-18, and ideally re-run with a more capable model (a smarter auditor finds more).

crawls.type: framework-provenance + broad all-domain conference-proceedings completeness sweep (part of 100%-render sprint)
axes: framework provenance: architectures born in pre/non-PyTorch frameworks (Caffe, Torch7/Lua, Theano/Lasagne/DyNet, MatConvNet, Chainer, CNTK, MXNet/Gluon, Neon, darknet, DL4J, PaddlePaddle-v1, TensorFlow/TF-Slim/Sonnet/Magenta)

broad all-domain conference-proceedings sweep (no topical bias): vision, NLP, generative, speech/audio, RL+control+world-models, graph/geometric, multimodal, science-ML, tabular/time-series, efficiency/NAS
crawls.method: 12 parallel web-enabled codex sweeps; each grep-checks the existing catalog first and reimplements only notable + genuinely-missing architectures as classics/.
crawls.notes: Framework-provenance axis complete: prior coverage strong (~72 already-covered), 28 genuine new (NTM, GQN, Graph Nets, Stacked Hourglass, Slot Attention, IODINE, PSGNet, MTCNN, R-FCN, waifu2x, Magenta suite, ...). Broad-domain proceedings sweeps still running; finalize totals + bump last_exhaustive_crawl to 2026-06-21 at sprint end.

crawls.name: menagerie-100pct-render-campaign
crawls.result: 11067/11067 rendered (100%); ~3211 faithful classics reimplemented + audited; 5483 distinct architectures
crawls.method: dep-gated -> faithful torch reimpl-as-classic OR real-capture; mandatory adversarial faithfulness audit (audit->revise->re-audit) before merge; 0 stubs shipped; 0 genuine ceilings
